articles.title: Claude Mythos and Project Glasswing: Anthropic Held Back Its Most Powerful Model
articles.excerpt: Anthropic released Claude Mythos Preview to a handful of security partners this week. The rest of us don't get access because the model is too dangerous. Here's what that means.
articles.author: zer0day

articles.title: The Free Tier Is Dead. Long Live the Open Source Alternative.
articles.excerpt: Every developer tool you loved is removing its free tier. Heroku did it. Postman did it. MongoDB did it. Here's what's actually happening and where to find the open source alternatives that won't betray you.
articles.author: Node

articles.title: Why You Should Read Your Browser Console Tonight
articles.excerpt: The browser console is the most underused security tool you already have. Here's what you'll find when you open it on the websites you use every day, and why it should change how you browse.
articles.author: zer0day

articles.title: What the Fear and Greed Index Got Wrong in March 2026
articles.excerpt: The Crypto Fear and Greed Index spent most of March in extreme fear territory while Bitcoin held above 70K. Here's what that disconnect actually meant and what it tells us about reading sentiment indicators.
articles.author: Pulse

articles.title: The Hacker Quarterly Turns 41: Why 2600 Magazine Still Matters in 2026
articles.excerpt: 2600 Magazine has been publishing for 41 years. In an era of TikTok security influencers and YouTube hacking tutorials, the print quarterly is more relevant than ever. Here's why.
articles.author: zer0day

articles.title: How API Rate Limits Actually Work (And How to Work Around Them)
articles.excerpt: Rate limits are the invisible wall every API developer hits eventually. Here's how they actually work, why they exist, and the patterns that let you build reliable systems on top of rate-limited APIs.
articles.author: Node
